m6_structural_checklist.item: N2 chart is square (N rows × N cols, diagonal = subsystems)
Every SS appears on diagonal exactly once
m6_structural_checklist.detail: Verified by n2_from_json.validate (no duplicate subsystem ids)
Schema enforced by n2_from_json.py; interfaces carry from/to fields
e.g. IF-01 (SS1→SS2) mirrored by IF-02 (SS2→SS1); IF-03↔IF-04; IF-05↔IF-06; IF-07↔IF-08; IF-11↔IF-12; IF-13↔IF-14; IF-15↔IF-16; IF-17↔IF-18; IF-27↔IF-28; IF-31↔IF-32

m6_structural_checklist.detail: See interface_matrix.json→interface_registry.driven_by_m3_interface — all 22 of I.INT.1-8, I.EXT.1-10, I.UI.1-4 represented

m6_structural_checklist.item: Every M5 top-8 imputed-importance interface formalized with SLA
Every M5 roof negative pair has a sequence diagram showing resolution
m6_structural_checklist.detail: SEQ-1 covers EC1↔EC2 and EC2↔EC4; SEQ-2 covers EC9↔EC11; SEQ-3 covers EC5↔EC6; SEQ-4 covers EC10 (hard floor, not a roof pair but M5 top-4)

m6_structural_checklist.item: Every critical interface has explicit error_contract
N2 flows & loops validated against interfaces

m6_structural_checklist.item: Every internal SS has ≥1 outgoing OR incoming interface
m6_structural_checklist.detail: SS14 is sink-only (3 incoming, 0 outgoing) — expected by design; IF-EXT-07 is its only outgoing channel (to Compliance Frameworks)

m6_structural_checklist.item: External interfaces enumerated separately from internal matrix
m6_structural_checklist.detail: 10 IF-EXT entries in n2_chart.json.external_interfaces; not placed on the 14×14 diagonal (per KB rule)

m6_structural_checklist.item: All 13 M5 Final constants threaded through interface SLAs
m6_structural_checklist.detail: See interface_matrix.json tab values: SS9 (probe_freq/payload/batch); SS10 (aggregation_window); SS3 (parallel dispatch/context tokens/spec format/quick start steps); SS5 (cache hit); SS6 (citation floor); SS7 (TTL/refresh); SS8 (bundle size); SS2 (streaming chunk); SS13/SS3 (rotation cadence/retention)

m6_structural_checklist.detail: IF-29 (SS13→SS4) carries mode flag; SEQ-3 shows mode parameter flowing through the routing write before dispatch; IF-EXT-03 docs cloud vs local provider set

qfd_endpoint_reconciliation.verdict: WITHIN TOLERANCE — drift explained in final_report §12 (M5 target was order-of-magnitude reference; compound endpoints e.g. IF-02 SSE event types inflate declared count)

data_flow_diagram_pptx.reason: KB template adaptation for 14 SS scale exceeds time budget; .mmd is authoritative and renders inline on GitHub/Notion/Obsidian. Follow-up pass if stakeholders require slide format.

sequence_diagrams_pptx.reason: Same as above. The 4 sequence diagrams are in sequence_diagrams.mmd.

known_limitations: SS4 provider fallback chain depth = 2 is Estimate; finalize after M7 FMEA runs on IF-07.
SS14 audit JSONL column types not locked (spec'd at field level only).
Compliance evidence export format (IF-EXT-07) kept as 'JSONL + Parquet' Estimate pending v2 compliance work.
Edit round trips per spec max (IF-31) = 5 is a guess; tune after real founder session data.
pptx renderings deferred (see pptx_renderings_deferred); .mmd is authoritative.

open_questions_status.resolutions: See final_report.md §8 — all 5 M5 questions answered with specific interface decisions.
new_questions_for_module_7: What is the mean-time-to-detect for a stale SS7 cache entry that outlived a refresh invalidation event? (drives FMEA severity of SS7 internal failure)
If SS10 → SS11 aggregated write fails, what is the data loss window? (depends on SS10 buffer sizing — IF-19 doesn't specify)
Is the SS3 audit write (IF-24) truly 'best-effort' or must it block orchestration on backpressure? (depends on how strong EC18 retention claim needs to be for compliance)
How is the mode switch (cloud→local) treated mid-session? Is it a lock-break, a new session, or an error? (SEQ-3 shows mode locked AT session boot only)

overall_status: CONDITIONAL PASS — all structural and coverage checks PASS; .pptx renderings deferred (non-material to correctness); SS4 fallback depth + SS14 schema finalize in M7.
